news.title: Wallabies vs Ireland live: Captain’s try scrubbed out in contentious obstruction call

sections.heading: Vakatawa set for Fiji debut in reshuffled backline
sections.body: Former France centre Virimi Vakatawa will make his Fiji debut in a revamped backline. Vakatawa joins Josua Tuisova in the midfield, while Caleb Muntz and Frank Lomani form the starting halfback pairing. Isaiah Armstrong-Ravula starts at fullback, with Selestino Ravutaumada and Jiuta Wainiqolo on the wings. The lineup aims to wash away the memories of last weekend's heavy defeat.

sections.heading: Camp focus shifts to defensive repair over medical concerns
sections.body: Fiji's medical updates remain absent from today's coverage, suggesting tactical preparation has taken centre stage. Having established their training camp in the United Kingdom for this opening block of the tournament, the squad has focused on defensive alignment. The coaching team has prioritised cohesion and physical recovery in training to address the defensive errors that proved costly during last week's loss.

sections.heading: Scotland make 14 starting changes for Murrayfield clash
sections.body: Scotland have made 14 changes, with only Gregor Brown retaining his place. Stafford McDowall captains the hosts. Scotland's head coach expects a battle, noting: “The breakdown is going to be a crucial area. Fiji love to jackal; we now have one of the best jackals in the world on our team, so if it becomes that sort of game, I think Freddy can come into his own.”

sections.heading: Fiji search for vital breakthrough to avoid winless summer
sections.body: Fiji started the round sitting 11th in the Nations Championship table, searching for their first points of the campaign. Securing a victory would not only spark their tournament but also replicate last year's 29-14 win over Scotland. However, another defeat would leave the Flying Fijians winless in the summer series before the tournament resumes in November.

sections.heading: Borthwick keeps faith in dual-Smith axis
sections.body: Steve Borthwick has opted for continuity following the demolition of Fiji, naming an unchanged backline. Fin Smith continues at fly-half with Marcus Smith at No. 15, while hat-trick hero Henry Pollock remains on a bench featuring midfield power Benhard Janse van Rensburg. The squad has notably avoided wearing Three Lions training kit in the capital this week to lower the temperature following Wednesday’s footballing heartbreak.

sections.heading: Selection indicates stable squad health
sections.body: The naming of an unchanged starting XV suggests the medical team has successfully managed the recovery of the senior core following the win over Fiji. Training in Argentina has focused on tactical discipline while maintaining a low profile in the city. With the same matchday squad retained, the coaching staff is prioritizing physical maintenance and set-piece stability over further rotation in the pack.

sections.heading: Montoya leads experienced Pumas lineup
sections.body: Argentina have made two changes to their starting side, with tighthead Tomás Rapetti and lock Matías Alemanno drafted into a heavy-duty pack. Captain Julián Montoya anchors a front row tasked with disrupting England’s rhythm at the scrum. The hosts are looking to snap a five-match losing streak against England, relying on the playmaking of Santiago Carreras to exploit any gaps in the visitors' defensive line.

sections.heading: England seek climb toward top four
sections.body: England goes into the weekend in eighth place, trailing Northern Hemisphere rivals Ireland, France, and Scotland. A victory in Santiago del Estero is vital for Borthwick’s side to maintain pressure on the leaders and build momentum following their Round 2 rebound. Defeat would likely see them drift into the bottom half of the log.

sections.heading: Virimi Vakatawa completes switch to Fiji
sections.body: The former France centre makes his international return today, but not in the blue jersey. After earning 32 caps for Les Bleus, Virimi Vakatawa has been named to debut for Fiji in their clash against Scotland. While France supporters remember his explosive line-breaking for the national side, his revival under the Fijian flag adds a new dimension to the upcoming November meeting.

sections.heading: Conditioning focus ahead of autumn window
sections.body: Today’s coverage provides no fresh injury updates from the French camp following the Round 3 victory over Japan. With the next fixture several months away, the squad's medical focus remains on monitoring long-term recovery rather than immediate match fitness. This quiet period allows the coaching staff to assess the heavy workload of the domestic season before reassembling the group for the autumn block.

sections.heading: Fiji hand debut to former France star
sections.body: Fiji take the field against Scotland today with a squad featuring familiar faces for French fans. Beyond Vakatawa’s debut, the Pacific side remains a dangerous prospect defined by the clinical sevens-style play that saw them reach the 2024 Olympic final. Looking ahead, the Islanders will seek to replicate their historic 2018 victory in Paris, where they punished French handling errors to win 21-14.

sections.heading: France hold steady in second place
sections.body: Following the convincing 42-15 win over Japan, France go into the weekend sitting second in the Nations Championship table. With two wins from three matches and a healthy points difference of +41, the side remains in the hunt for the top spot. A positive result in the next round is essential to maintain pressure on the leaders as the tournament enters its final stages.

sections.heading: Squad depth under focus
sections.body: Ireland head into this fixture facing notable personnel challenges. Coverage confirms that a key quartet is ruled out of the current Nations Championship. With the team aiming to address areas of inconsistency following recent performances, the coaching staff is managing a depleted group. Preparations are focused on securing a positive result in the tournament after the recent loss to New Zealand.

sections.heading: Managing current absences
sections.body: Current reports confirm that captain Caelan Doris is among those ruled out of the Nations Championship. Beyond the specific unavailability of the established core group mentioned in media coverage, there is no fresh injury news provided for the remainder of the squad. Medical staff continue to monitor the conditioning of all available players as the competition continues.

sections.heading: Pumas remain a growing threat
sections.body: Argentina head coach Felipe Contepomi describes rugby union as booming in his country. The Pumas are currently involved in the Nations Championship schedule, with recent fixtures including an encounter with England. Their development as a competitive force in the international game remains a primary talking point for analysts assessing the current southern hemisphere tour block.

sections.heading: Points table outlook
sections.body: Ireland enter the upcoming fixture with a record of two wins and one loss from three matches played. Having accumulated 90 points for and 91 against, the side sits with a points difference of -1. Maintaining discipline and improving performance levels remain the primary objectives as the campaign progresses toward the later rounds of the inaugural Nations Championship.

sections.heading: Azzurri look to regroup after Australian defeat
sections.body: Following a difficult outing against Australia where the side conceded 57 points, the focus for the Italy camp remains on structural improvements in defense and transition play. With no formal squad update released today, management continues to evaluate personnel rotations to address the current points-against deficit. The primary objective remains finding the necessary clinical edge to compete against high-tempo, physical opponents.

sections.heading: Medical staff monitoring squad recovery
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage following the conclusion of the third round. Medical staff are currently focused on general player conditioning and managing the physical toll of the recent heavy-scoring fixtures. Coaches have not indicated any new absences, with all players who featured against Australia remaining under standard observation as the squad cycles through its recovery protocols.

sections.heading: Springboks maintain dominant historical form
sections.body: South Africa enters this period as a formidable challenge, noted for their relentless forward pressure and strike capability across the pitch. Recent encounters have seen the Springboks utilize their deep bench to maintain intensity, underscored by a recent 45-0 victory where they demonstrated defensive discipline even when reduced to 14 men. The squad continues to rely on high-impact carries and well-drilled set-piece execution.

sections.heading: Refining performance as the tournament continues
sections.body: Italy remains at the base of the table following three consecutive losses, having struggled to contain opponents defensively so far. While the immediate goal is to secure a first win of the campaign, the upcoming match against the Springboks serves as a vital test of the team's resilience. The focus remains on accumulating points and tightening the defensive structure to improve tournament standing.

sections.heading: Townsend rotates for Murrayfield finale
sections.body: Gregor Townsend has made 14 changes to his starting XV for today’s fixture against Fiji. Gregor Brown is the only player retained from the side that faced South Africa, shifting from the second row to number eight. Stafford McDowall takes the captaincy in a team selection the head coach says is aimed at “creating depth” as Scotland concludes its summer campaign.

sections.heading: Macbeth sidelined by leg infection
sections.body: Nathan Macbeth is the only player ruled out of today's Test after developing a leg infection in recent days. Townsend confirmed that apart from Macbeth, every player who traveled on tour is available for selection today. The squad has been training throughout the summer series, ensuring those who did not feature against South Africa or Argentina are prepared for this final opportunity.

sections.heading: Fiji debuts Vakatawa in Edinburgh
sections.body: Fiji has named their starting XV with Virimi Vakatawa set to make his debut for the visitors at Murrayfield. The Flying Fijians are seeking their first tournament win after heavy losses to Wales and England. Townsend has highlighted the breakdown as a crucial area, noting that Fiji “love to jackal” and expects a high-tempo, competitive contest as the visitors look to recover form.

sections.heading: Building momentum for the autumn
sections.body: Today’s match marks the final summer engagement for Scotland in the Nations Championship. Following last week’s spirited performance against the world champions, this result serves as a vital marker for the squad’s future depth. While the tournament pauses after this round, a victory today would ensure the side carries essential confidence and competitive momentum into the upcoming autumn fixtures.

sections.heading: Lake captains side in Durban
sections.body: Dewi Lake leads the side at Hollywoodbets Kings Park as Wales look to rebound following their Round 2 defeat to Argentina. Steve Tandy has opted for an experienced backline featuring Louis Rees-Zammit, Josh Adams, and Ben Thomas, while the forward pack sees Rhys Carre and Dillon Lewis anchoring the front row for this afternoon's meeting with the world champions.

sections.heading: Squad status remains stable
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding the traveling squad. Following the physical encounter in the second round, the medical staff has focused on conditioning and recovery. The lineup confirmed for today shows no late changes, with the named replacements expected to provide impact during the final quarter of the match.

sections.heading: Springboks name powerful starting XV
sections.body: South Africa enters the contest with a formidable lineup, including Pieter-Steph du Toit, who retains the captaincy, and a sharp backline directed by Cobus Reinach. Assistant coach Mzwandile Stick recently noted that Wales are currently in a similar transition phase to where the Springboks were in 2018, adding that for the visitors, one or two good results might change everything for them.

sections.heading: Seeking a vital result in Round 3
sections.body: Wales sits with 5 points in the Northern Hemisphere log heading into this afternoon's challenge. A victory against the top-ranked side would serve as a major turning point after a difficult run of results. Conversely, the Springboks are looking to maintain their unbeaten start to the tournament and strengthen their current position at the top of the Southern Hemisphere standings.

sections.heading: Two adjustments for the Pumas
sections.body: Argentina enters this contest with two changes to the starting lineup that secured the recent win over Wales. The reshuffle sees one alteration within the forward pack and one in the backs as the coaching staff looks to maintain momentum. The squad will aim to improve their historical standing against the visitors, currently seeking a rare victory in a rivalry that has favoured England heavily since 1981.

sections.heading: Squad status remains stable
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage following the victory against Wales. The Pumas appear to be maintaining their current squad availability as they prepare to face an England side that has remained unchanged in their starting XV. The medical and conditioning teams continue to monitor the group closely ahead of the intensity expected in Santiago del Estero this evening.

sections.heading: Unchanged England side arrives
sections.body: England head coach Steve Borthwick has named an unchanged starting XV for this encounter, while opting for two alterations on the bench with the inclusion of Ben Spencer and Emmanuel Iyogun. Reflecting on the upcoming fixture, Borthwick said: "We're excited by the challenge of playing Los Pumas in Santiago del Estero." The visitors are eager to build on their dominant win against Fiji last weekend.

sections.heading: Targeting a climb up the table
sections.body: Following a solid result against Wales, the team occupies the middle of the log and seeks to build on their single win from two matches. With the tournament standings currently led by Ireland, tonight’s result is pivotal for Argentina to stay competitive. A win would provide significant momentum, while England arrives hoping to extend their winning streak in this historic, often one-sided series between the two nations.

match.venue: Estadio Único Madre de Ciudades, Santiago del Estero

sections.heading: Wallabies look to build momentum
sections.body: Australia enters the weekend in a positive mood following a dominant 57-10 victory over Italy in Round 3. With the squad showing newfound attacking rhythm, the focus remains on maintaining this clinical form as the side balances consistency with long-term tactical development. The coaching staff continues to evaluate player rotations, aiming to sustain performance levels heading into the later stages of the Nations Championship.

sections.heading: Medical update pending
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding the Wallabies squad. Medical staff continue to monitor the conditioning of the group following the physical demands of the recent win against Italy. Players remain under routine observation as the team maintains their preparation cycle, with no new availability concerns reported to the public ahead of the next round of tournament fixtures.

sections.heading: England facing identity search
sections.body: England remains under scrutiny regarding their tactical direction as coach Steve Borthwick seeks a clearer identity. While the squad recently underwent a review following a challenging Six Nations campaign, they are currently focused on their upcoming performance against Argentina. The English side continues to rely heavily on set-piece stability and a defensive game plan, attributes that have defined their approach throughout Borthwick’s tenure.

sections.heading: Tournament positioning tightens
sections.body: The Wallabies hold a 1-2 record in the competition, currently sitting with a positive points difference of 29. Every result in the current round influences the live standings, as teams jostle for position in the middle of the log. Securing points against future opponents will be critical for Australia to climb the table and solidify their standing within this inaugural Nations Championship.

sections.heading: Looking to bounce back after Paris
sections.body: Japan heads into the next block of the championship aiming to recalibrate following the 42-15 defeat to France in the previous round. The squad continues to integrate lessons from that loss as they refine their high-tempo game plan. Staff will be focused on tightening defensive structures and improving composure during the transition phases to avoid conceding points in clusters against top-tier opposition.

sections.heading: Monitoring fitness across the squad
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage following the conclusion of the France fixture. Medical staff are continuing to monitor the physical conditioning of the group as they manage the cumulative fatigue of the campaign. The focus remains on ensuring optimal recovery protocols are met, keeping the core personnel available for the upcoming challenge against the Welsh side in the next fixture.

sections.heading: Wales eyes momentum following past clashes
sections.body: Wales remains a dangerous, tactical opponent known for their physical persistence. Having secured a dramatic 24-23 victory in their most recent meeting in Cardiff, they will be aiming to build upon that result when they travel to meet Japan in the next round. Their coaching staff continues to emphasize clinical execution, particularly in the final quarter, where they have previously found late points against the Blossoms.

sections.heading: Securing crucial points in the table
sections.body: Japan sits 10th in the log with a 1-2 record. With two losses and a solitary win, the upcoming Test serves as a vital opportunity to gain ranking points and improve the current points difference of -26. A victory is essential to climbing the table and establishing a more stable foundation for the remainder of the 2026 Nations Championship campaign.

sections.heading: Maintaining momentum after Italy
sections.body: Following a dominant 47-17 victory over Italy, the squad enters the current break with the coaching staff focused on refining set-piece efficiency. The team has utilised this period to consolidate their tactical framework, prioritising conditioning to ensure they remain fresh for the remainder of the Nations Championship. With two wins from two, the focus remains on maintaining high standards of execution across the full 80 minutes.

sections.heading: Camp monitoring squad fitness
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage following the win against Italy. The medical team continues to monitor players through routine load management and rehabilitation protocols as the side prepares for the upcoming block of fixtures. The current squad remains available for selection, with conditioning staff reporting positive trends as the group works through tactical drills during this training phase.

sections.heading: Scotland's physical approach
sections.body: Scotland remains a dangerous side, particularly when relying on their set-piece power and maul threat. Their forwards proved persistent in recent fixtures, consistently challenging opponents through high-impact phases. As we look toward our encounter in November, the focus will be on nullifying their ability to grind out territory and controlling the breakdown to prevent their pack from building the momentum required to execute their driving game.

sections.heading: Building on early success
sections.body: Having secured two victories to date, the team occupies a strong position as the tournament progresses. Every match in the Nations Championship remains critical, and maintaining this winning trajectory is essential to stay at the front of the pack. With the points difference currently at plus 32, the upcoming fixtures will be vital in cementing our standing for the final rounds of competition.

sections.heading: Springboks name experienced XV for Durban finale
sections.body: South Africa enters today’s encounter at Hollywoodbets Kings Park with a settled, experienced lineup. The squad features mainstays including Malcolm Marx, Pieter-Steph du Toit, Damian de Allende, and Jesse Kriel. Assistant coach Mzwandile Stick has expressed confidence in the current group, noting that the management team continues to draw on the lessons learned during their own transition period in 2018 to build a world-class system.

sections.heading: Dewi Lake sidelined for visiting side
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news regarding the Springbok camp in today’s coverage, suggesting the squad is ready for the Round 3 fixture. Conversely, Wales will be without their captain, Dewi Lake, who is set to miss the match. His absence is a significant blow for the visitors as they prepare to face the world champions on South African soil this afternoon.

sections.heading: Wales face uphill battle against world champions
sections.body: Steve Tandy’s Wales side arrives in Durban following a Round 2 loss to Argentina, having opened their campaign with a win over Fiji. Mzwandile Stick recently compared the current Welsh struggle to the Springboks' own challenges in 2018. The visitors look to captain Jac Morgan and veterans Josh Adams, Tomos Williams, and Louis Rees-Zammit to provide the stability required to challenge the tournament leaders.

sections.heading: Boks look to maintain perfect tournament record
sections.body: South Africa enters today’s match aiming to preserve their unbeaten status in the Nations Championship. Following commanding victories over England and Scotland, the team currently sits among the leaders in the Southern Hemisphere standings. A successful result today would further solidify their position at the top of the World Rugby rankings and continue their current five-match winning streak across all international competitions.
